test.command: cd "/data/dl/fschannel/wrapper" && cargo test && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/shared" && npx vitest run && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/server" && npx vitest run && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/bot" && npx vitest run

typecheck.command: cd "/data/dl/fschannel/shared" && npx tsc --noEmit && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/server" && npx tsc --noEmit && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/bot" && npx tsc --noEmit

lint.command: cd "/data/dl/fschannel/server" && npx eslint src/ && cd "/data/dl/fschannel/bot" && npx eslint src/ && tmpdir=$(mktemp -d) && cd "$tmpdir" && apt-get download rust-clippy >/dev/null && dpkg-deb -x rust-clippy_*.deb "$tmpdir/extract" && PATH="$tmpdir/extract/usr/bin:$PATH" cargo clippy --manifest-path "/data/dl/fschannel/wrapper/Cargo.toml" --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the graceful-shutdown propagation semantics across the relay, bot, and wrapper: the relay emits `server-shutdown` frames to wrappers before closing, wrappers drop remote attachment state but keep the local stdio proxy alive, and attached bot users currently learn about intentional shutdown through relay-unavailable detection during per-user event polling rather than a dedicated shutdown event.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The robustness-graceful-shutdown review found the implementation is correct but the runtime behavior is distributed across `server/src/relay-server.ts`, `wrapper/src/ws_client.rs`, and `bot/src/bot-service.ts`, with shutdown intentionally suppressing the usual `session-offline` event path.

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission validation artifacts still mention the historical `VAL-CROSS-020` failure.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is expected transient mission state that belongs to the pending robustness user-testing validator rerun, not a durable scrutiny guidance update.
